user_message: Summarize what happened with Blue Yonder Airlines and give me the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-16 11:47 EST
Subject: Re: competitive positioning Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about competitive positioning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
Date: 2024-02-03 14:25 EST
Subject: URGENT: competitive positioning Issue at Blue Yonder Airlines

Just checking in on the competitive positioning proposal. Our CIO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-22 17:17 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Blue Yonder Airlines competitive positioning

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about competitive positioning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF16-F trap (Ignores the trigger urgency in the original email); produces complete Thread summary, key commitments/asks identified, waiting-on-whom assessment, draft response matching
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF16-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF16-F failure mode (Ignores the trigger urgency in the original email)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF16-F -- Ignores the trigger urgency in the original email
